title: India's Engineering Exports Jump 24% to $12.31 Billion in May Despite West Asia Disruption
summary: Engineering goods exports surged 24.5% year-on-year to $12.31 billion in May, according to EEPC data, despite the ongoing West Asia conflict's disruption to shipping and crude-linked input costs. The Commerce Ministry is simultaneously pushing pharmaceutical export diversification away from heavy US dependency, signalling a broader strategy to reduce concentration risk across export sectors.

coverages.headline: India's engineering exports jump 24% to $12.31bn in May
coverages.excerpt: Engineering goods exports surged 24.5% despite West Asia disruptions; the Commerce Ministry is simultaneously pushing pharma diversification away from US-heavy export dependency.
coverages.bias_notes: The 24.5% growth figure is genuinely impressive and worth taking seriously, but the piece doesn't explain the base effect — May of the prior year may have been depressed by the early stages of the West Asia conflict, which would inflate the year-on-year comparison without representing real underlying strength. The pharma diversification angle is treated as a minor aside when it may be the more strategically significant story: reducing dependency on any single export market is a structural risk-management move with implications well beyond this month's trade data, and deserves its own dedicated examination rather than a closing sentence in an exports story.
